summary:
Comprehensive backend + frontend testing for Kokowa Labs strategic advisory site. All 8 backend pytest tests pass; all 18 frontend test points pass (one false-positive on text matching due to
tag). Hero headline matches exactly, no em-dashes detected, multi-step apply form submits successfully and redirects to /thank-you with id, footer/header content correct, all routes load without 404.
critical_code_review_comments: Code is clean, well-structured. Backend uses UUID id field (not Mongo _id) and excludes _id on reads — good. Pydantic EmailStr validation works correctly returning 422. Custom 400 for short challenge works as designed.

Minor: server.py defines logger AFTER it is referenced inside create_application's webhook except block (line 121). If MAKE_WEBHOOK_URL is set and the request fails, this would raise NameError. Recommend moving logging.basicConfig + logger = logging.getLogger(__name__) above the route definitions.
Apply.jsx submit() does not retry on network errors — acceptable for MVP.
test_credentials: Not applicable (public site, no auth flows for end users). Admin GET /api/applications correctly returns 401 without ADMIN_TOKEN env var.
seed_data_creation: 2 TEST_-prefixed application records created via end-to-end form submission and direct API. Data persists in MongoDB applications collection. Recommend periodic cleanup.
context_for_next_testing_agent:
Backend test file at /app/backend/tests/test_kokowa_api.py covers POST /api/applications validation (200/400/422), GET /api/applications auth (401), root endpoint, persistence/no _id leakage. Frontend Playwright flow validates: hero headline exact match, TM symbol count=1, em-dash count=0, all nav links + routes (/approach, /work-with-us, /insights, /about, /apply, /thank-you), full 7-step apply form submission with redirect, validation on empty step 0, footer founder credit and TM. Note: 'The Rules Have Changed' is split with
so any text-content matching must use partial matches.
rca_of_the_issue:
No bugs found. The single 'failed' phrase check ('The Rules Have Changed') is a test-side false-positive: NewGame.jsx renders 'The Rules
Have Changed.' so innerText contains a newline between 'Rules' and 'Have'. Visual content is correct.
